brief.title: aion-awl: parser + canonical printer with lossless round-trip
brief.objective: Extend crates/aion-awl with a parser producing a spanned Document AST for the full AWL-0 grammar, and a canonical printer, such that printing is idempotent (print . parse . print = print) and the research_report fixture round-trips byte-identically (print(parse(fixture)) == fixture, comments included).
brief.context: The lexer landed in AWL-1 (crates/aion-awl/src/lib.rs, aion_awl::lex -> Vec<Token> with spans, Indent/Dedent, Prose mode for about lines). The grammar contract is docs/design/aion-authoring/awl/AWL-0-SPEC-DRAFT.md (Document grammar + Step fields + Expressions sections) and the worked example is the code block in docs/design/aion-authoring/syntax-sketches/H-awl.md, already stored as crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/research_report.awl. Declarations in order: workflow, about, input (repeatable), output, error (optional), signal (repeatable), type (repeatable, nominal record { field: Type, ... }), action (repeatable, with optional indented routing fields queue/node/timeout/retry), step (repeatable, ordered), finish <expr>. Step fields, one per line: about (Prose), when <expr>, each <id> in <expr>, do <call> or do child <workflow>(args), wait <signal>, sleep <duration>, repeat up to <expr>, until <expr>, retry <n> every <d> or retry <n> backoff <d>..<d>, timeout <duration>, on timeout / on failure handler blocks (indented one further: do lines then finish <expr> or fail), as <name>, queue "<q>", node "<n>". A step does exactly one of: do, wait, sleep (enforce in the parser with a spanned error). Expression micro-grammar: literals (string/int/float/bool/duration), list literals [a, b], references, field access (approval.ok, chained), record construction TypeName(field: expr, ...), calls ONLY as the do target, not/and/or (standard precedence: not > and > or), comparisons (== != < <= > >=, non-associative), string +. Types in signatures: TitleCase names, List(T), Option(T), and the builtins Bool/Int/Float/String/Nil/Dir. COMMENT PRESERVATION: the fixture contains // comments (own-line and trailing); the current lexer may discard them. Round-tripping requires retaining them, so extending the lexer to emit comment/trivia tokens (or capture trivia on adjacent tokens) is IN SCOPE this brief; keep the public lex API shape (aion_awl::lex(source) -> Result<Vec<Token>, LexError>) backward compatible. PRINTER DISCIPLINE (the hard requirement a prior attempt failed): the Document AST MUST NOT retain the raw source text, source byte ranges used for re-emission, or any per-node copy of original formatting; print renders purely from AST structure plus attached comment trivia. Echoing input bytes is an automatic reject. The hand-written fixture's ad-hoc trailing-comment column alignment is NOT canonical and will not survive printing — that is correct behavior. Per the spec, documents are byte-stable AFTER ONE FORMAT PASS: the golden is normalize-then-idempotence, not byte-preserving the hand-written file. Canonical comment rendering: an own-line comment prints on its own line at the indentation of the item it precedes; a trailing comment prints on the same line as its code, separated by exactly two spaces.
scope_out: No typechecker, no action-contract checking, no binding-flow analysis — that is AWL-3
No projection to the aion-package WorkflowGraph model yet
No changes to any other crate and no changes to the workspace Cargo.toml
Do not modify the spec or sketch documents
Do not rename or break the existing public lex API
acceptance: aion_awl::parse(source: &str) -> Result<Document, ParseError> (or Vec<ParseError>; document the choice) covers the full AWL-0 grammar: every declaration kind, every step field, handler blocks, and the complete expression micro-grammar; every AST node carries a span

aion_awl::print(&Document) -> String emits exactly one canonical rendering: declarations in canonical order as written, step fields one per line in the spec's canonical field order, two-space indentation, comments preserved as trivia with the canonical comment rendering from the context

ANTI-ECHO, asserted by tests: (a) the Document type stores no source text or source-slice references for re-emission (grep-level review criterion for the lenses); (b) two differently-formatted sources of the SAME document (e.g. the fixture and a comment-realigned/extra-blank-line variant) print to the identical string; (c) a test that parses, mutates the AST in code (e.g. renames a step and changes a duration), then prints, sees both mutations in the output

Fixture golden (normalize-then-idempotence): crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/research_report.awl parses with zero errors; its canonical rendering is committed as crates/aion-awl/tests/fixtures/research_report.canonical.awl; a golden test asserts print(parse(research_report.awl)) == that canonical file, that parse(canonical) equals parse(fixture) structurally (ignoring spans), that print(parse(canonical)) == canonical byte-identically, and that every comment from the fixture appears in the canonical output

A second fixture exercises the bounded-cycle shape from H-awl.md (step with repeat up to <expr> / do child fix_round(...) / as state / until state.accepted) plus retry backoff d..d and an error declaration; same normalize-then-idempotence golden treatment
Printer idempotence: for both fixtures and at least three hand-written non-canonical-whitespace variants, print(parse(print(parse(src)))) == print(parse(src))

Parse errors carry correct spans and messages, each unit-tested: a step with two world-touching fields (do + wait), finish missing at document end, a call expression outside do, an unknown step field, an unterminated record construction, wrong indentation depth for a handler block body
The step-shape rule is enforced structurally: each/repeat bodies are exactly one do (the field-per-line grammar makes this implicit — assert the two-do error covers it)
cargo test -p aion-awl passes; clippy -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --all leaves the tree unchanged
brief.notes: Hand-written recursive-descent parser over the AWL-1 token stream; no parser generators. Keep the AST plain data (public fields or simple accessors) — the AWL-3 typechecker and the future graph projection both walk it. Printer discipline: there is no configuration and no check mode; the printer IS the formatter, and it renders from the AST alone — never from retained source. If the current lexer's comment handling blocks trivia attachment, extend the lexer minimally (trivia tokens or attached trivia) rather than post-processing strings in the printer. A prior attempt was rejected for source-echoing print; the branch dev/AWL-2-salvage exists as reference for its parser shape only — do not imitate its printer.
